name: Persistent Wiki Pages
description: Durable, revision-tracked, human-override-safe wiki pages. Three tables (wiki_pages, wiki_sections, wiki_section_revisions) plus three RPCs (wiki_upsert_page, wiki_write_section, wiki_accept_pending) give each page per-section ownership and an append-only history. The headline is the regen guard: a machine write to a human-owned section parks a pending draft instead of overwriting it, and a human accepts pending via wiki_accept_pending — same trust model as a human-in-the-loop. UUID id chains throughout (wiki_sections.page_id and wiki_section_revisions.section_id are UUID FKs; evidence_thought_ids is UUID[] referencing public.thoughts). Distinct from the wiki-compiler / wiki-synthesis recipes, which compile throwaway markdown artifacts with no persistence or override guard.
